---
# Scheduled-task creation/exec (persistence)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Detect scheduled-task abuse: PROCESS events where the actor is `schtasks.exe`/`at.exe` OR any command line `contains "schtasks"`. Keep `causality_actor_process_image_name` to see what created the task — a task registered by Office, a script host, or a freshly-dropped binary is high-signal T1053.005 persistence. Extend the `contains` set with `"/create"` to narrow to creation (vs query/run).

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and (actor_process_image_name in ("schtasks.exe", "at.exe") or actor_process_command_line contains "schtasks")
| fields _time, actor_process_image_name, actor_process_command_line, causality_actor_process_image_name
```

_Query syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (narrow 30-min window). Adapt thresholds + the `contains`/`in` sets to your environment; filter early to keep the scan cheap._
